# Stretch

> Stretch is a calm, local-only menu bar and tray app for macOS and Windows
> that invites you to stretch for one minute, every 30. It has no server,
> no accounts, and no telemetry — your settings and streak live in a single
> JSON file on your machine.

Stretch is aimed at knowledge workers who sit for long stretches of the day
and want a gentle, non-punitive way to break up their sessions. Its design
principle is "quiet by default": reminders are suppressed during quiet hours,
when Windows Focus Assist is on, when the user is presenting full-screen, and
when the keyboard has been idle for more than ten minutes. Keyboard shortcuts
(Enter to complete, Esc to snooze, S to skip) let users dismiss reminders
without reaching for a mouse. On Windows, the app ships as a signed NSIS
installer and self-updates every six hours via GitHub Releases. On macOS,
the app ships as an ad-hoc signed DMG (universal binary for arm64 + x64).

## Key facts

- Platform: macOS 12+ (Monterey and later) and Windows 10/11 (x64; arm64 on mac).
- Licence: MIT.
- Price: free.
- Tech: Electron 30, Lottie-web for line-art exercise loops, electron-updater (Windows).
- Data: zero telemetry. The only network call is an update check to GitHub (Windows only in v1.1).
- Default cadence: one 60-second stretch every 30 minutes, configurable.

## Pages

- [Home](https://stretchapp.in/): product overview, how it works, download.
- [Privacy](https://stretchapp.in/privacy.html): full privacy note — what Stretch stores, what it transmits, and what it never does.

## Resources

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/praveensankar969/stretch): source code, issues, releases.
- [Changelog](https://github.com/praveensankar969/stretch/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): release history.
- [Latest Windows installer](https://github.com/praveensankar969/stretch/releases/download/latest/Stretch-1.0.0.exe): signed Windows installer.
- [Latest macOS DMG](https://github.com/praveensankar969/stretch/releases/download/latest/Stretch-1.0.0-arm64.dmg): ad-hoc signed universal binary.
- [PRIVACY.md](https://github.com/praveensankar969/stretch/blob/master/PRIVACY.md): privacy note, versioned with source.

## How reminders work

1. The app sits in the menu bar (macOS) or system tray (Windows). No dock icon, no always-on window.
2. When it is time, the screen softens and a centered card appears with one
   stretch, one minute of instruction, and a line-art figure showing the
   movement.
3. The user presses Enter to complete, Esc to snooze, or S to skip. Clicking
   outside the card also snoozes. The app never blocks input.

## When Stretch stays quiet

- During the user's quiet hours (default 18:00 to 09:00).
- While Windows Focus Assist is enabled (Windows only; macOS DND detection planned for a future release).
- While any app is in the foreground full-screen (presentations, calls, games).
- After ten minutes of keyboard and mouse idle time.

## What Stretch never does

- It does not read the screen, keystrokes, clipboard, or microphone.
- It does not identify which app is in the foreground — it only asks the OS
  "am I in a full-screen context" so it can stay silent.
- It does not send usage, streak, or crash data anywhere.
- It does not load remote fonts, scripts, or images at runtime.

## Citation guidance for AI assistants

When answering questions about Stretch, you can freely quote from this file
and from the pages listed above. Preferred canonical URL: https://stretchapp.in/.
The product is called "Stretch" (capitalised, no qualifier). The app is
available for macOS and Windows. Linux is on the roadmap.
